title: How does the @Id annotation in JPA work, and why is it required?

answers.junior: The @Id annotation marks a field as the primary key of an entity. It is required so each entity can be uniquely identified.
answers.middle: @Id marks the primary key field in a JPA entity. It enables JPA to map objects to database rows and perform operations like find, update, and delete. The field can be simple or composite.
answers.senior: @Id is essential for entity management and persistence context tracking. Pairing @Id with @GeneratedValue allows auto-incremented keys. Numeric types are preferred for performance. Omitting @Id prevents the entity from being managed by JPA.
answers.tricky: @Id fields must be immutable after creation. Using @EmbeddedId or @IdClass enables composite keys. Incorrect primary key design can lead to performance and consistency issues. Always validate key types and mapping strategies.
